author: @Boz.n.Bertha
text:
Like Robin Williams and so many more, this video should serve as a reminder to check in on your depressed friends more often.
those of us that suffer are masters at masking the internal pain and "putting on a brave face".
yeah, i'm fine! (when we are anything but...)
i don't know how many times i've watched this last video from our beloved Robert, but i cannot detect the pain we know was there, hidden behind that cheeky smile he always had.
his videos have started popping up on my feed again and whilst i admit, i was less engaged previously, now i want to appreciate his channel all the more because lets face it, it is brilliant and informative and we all mourn the loss of a Great British tinkerer  :(

author: @dennissorensen8765
text:
This video in itself, is a testament to the thoughtfulness and kindness of a great man. To think that Rob respectfully wanted to give hos half a million followers some kind of peaceful ending to what is probably the best educational channel of all, still to this day, before ending his own story. That was - to me - an incredible sense of obligation and regard for the rest of us, even in the depth of his own sadness. 

I actually miss him and would have loved for him to have found his happiness again, so he would have stayed among the living a lot longer. 

I actually often think of Rob. He was an enormous inspiration for me.

May his soul be at ease, wherever he is. ♥️🕯️

author: @gazsto
text:
In Loving Memory of Robert Murray-Smith

We gather to remember
Robert Murray-Smith—
An inventor in mind,
A creator in spirit,
A man who saw possibility
Where others saw the ordinary.

Through curiosity, skill, and quiet determination,
He left the world changed in ways
Both seen and unseen.

Though he has laid down his tools,
His ideas, his influence,
And the love he shared
Continue to live on in us.

May he rest in peace,
And may his legacy endure.

I have just discovered (after 3 video's) tonight Robert's very instructive and creative tutorials/explanations and I can't believe what I see here and saw right before this video with Robert's brother video.
As all I am shocked and very saddened by his loss. 

Mental health is a big thing. Everybody should be able to talk about it openly without being judged. Communication is a must. Robert will definitely be very missed. Hopefully his work and content will be preserved and accessible to all online. That way Robert Murray Smith (1963 - 2025) will be remembered and thanked for his great contribution.

author: @sioul2006
text:
Hello Robert, Hello Dave if you’ll read this. I can’t even emphasize how sorry I feel for you… If y’all want to understand what great impact Robert had on the life’s of people in his community: I’m an electrical engineering student and we learn a lot about theory but really not much practical stuff in university. Just yesterday I found myself in need of a high current source. I had an old transformer out of a dead microwave and needed to remove the secondary winding. Anyways, guess who was there to help me, to guide a young man through the process how to safely remove that winding without damaging the valuable parts, which isn’t trivial. 
It wasn’t my dad, not a professor from university. It was Robert. Robert helped me yesterday in his usually cheerful manner. Made me laugh and taught me a skill for life that’ll never ever go away. I just need to emphasize the value of the knowledge you gifted to the world, to anyone who was willing to receive the gift. 

Robert, you will be missed so much. I see a lot of people here who really loved you and it makes me sick to hear how much you must’ve suffered on the inside. You didn’t deserve to go through that. You didn’t deserve to have the feeling, you must bear the weight of all of that alone! 

You’ll teach on forever. That way a part of you will always live on. They say you die twice. Once your heart beats and the second time someone thinks about you for the last time. Even tho your first death was too soon, in terms of your second you’ll outlive so many of us!

I really hope that you found peace!

From Germany 

Louis

text: Sir Robert, the Tears are building up. I have been following you from the start. It been a long time and you have educated me on so many things. The Graphene, was what truly go me excited. I have been following and doing the experiments, buying kits, to learn more and found a 100MMT Graphite deposit. I wanted to build the Future and now you are no longer with it. Its been 2 weeks since I learned about your passing. I was upset knowing this was your last video. Yet it turned out to be your last days with us. I fully understand, and life is difficult at the best of times. You will never be forgotten. You have inspired so many people, around the world. To look at the world around us. To capture the energy and build something for making a better world.  I hope your site never gets taken down, and that someone that is dedicated, can take over, maintain and continue in your foot steps. 2 days ago i was reintroduced to this channel by the algorithm.
I have watched almost every video this man has made.
Because of circumstances i did not keep up with new videos for a few months.

It breaks my heart and tears fall down.
What a loss.
What a great guy.

I agree with everyone who says this channel should stay up. Even if there wont ever be another video.

author: @markdewdney9370
text:
Do not stand at my grave and weep
I am not there. I do not sleep.
I am a thousand winds that blow.
I am the diamond glints on snow.
I am the sunlight on ripened grain.
I am the gentle autumn rain.
When you awaken in the morning's hush
I am the swift uplifting rush
Of quiet birds in circled flight.
I am the soft stars that shine at night.
Do not stand at my grave and cry;
I am not there. I did not die.

author: @highlandermachineworks5795
text:
In regards to the news. I 100% suspected that Robert was suffering from something dark. (Having been in the medical field I've unfortunately seen the signs. And in the videos you could pick up on them.) What I'm more upset about is that, he did what he did. It is not a solution.
If you're in the same state of mind, go get help. It's free in many places. You don't have to  do extreme things.
I was never a regular viewer of the channel. Haven't watched in quite some time. But today i thought, I would look some things up and thic channel was in the results. I watched the video. Then i figured that I would see if he's done anything else interesting in recent times. And well I was surprised to see this video and the more recent one. 
I'm gonna say it again. 
Get help!! You're also hurting everyone who cares about you if you don't. 
There are hotlines to call.

author: @harrisonpike4168
text:
I am a fan through and through. Your wisdom and means of sharing it are a legacy greater than any individual I know. 

I am very sad to learn of your passing and would like to personally give thanks to your brother for his wise and generous video, sharing your passing with us all. Thank you from all of Robert’s fans. Thank you to Robert, for being such an inspiration to so many. 

Growing up, many struggle to find someone to spark their curiosity in a way that Robert would do so. From the moment I came across this channel, I knew I had found something special. Your platform emanated the energy you were so keen to share all with all who would listen. I will continue to speak your name in your honour, and in the spirit of an education made fun. 

Back into the void we all go, when our energy has been spent. You shone the brightest, until your flame went out.

Thank you again. And goodbye to an idol of mine, and a great many more.

author: @matildastanford7019
text:
😥😥😥😥😥
oh Rob,   Rest In Peace mate,   you're already greatly missed 'cause l, for one cannot speak for anyone else,  do believe that you had so much more to inspire us all with,  just wish you had taken a break until you got your mojo back.
The world needs more people like you Rob and l wish that we could've foreseen and said something that prevented you from taking your own life.

I do understand why you did what you did,  l nursed my former fiance for 9 months thru all his rapid deterioration & suffering until he ultimately died of melonoma cancer.
A vast piece of me died with him.
That happened on 16th of June 2004 and l'm still devastated by his loss to this day.
l'd like to say time heals those wounds & that it gets better,  but it doesn't.
R.I.P my darling Paul Craig Stephenson 💔

Needless to say that l've had many many dark days & nights drowning in an endless grief and despair,  even before meeting my soulmate.  But in those times l also consider that without my life there's one less loved one to honour his memory and life.

To anyone who's so deep within the vortex of the endless helpless depression where suicide seems to be the only answer to end all the suffering,   please bear in mind that you're not alone,  that there are others who love you (even if you cannot see it right now)  whose lives will be infinitely worse if you take yours and that taking your own life is the worst permanent solution to your temporary problems.
lt is true that you might never fully recover from all that you've lost,  but no one lives forever and inevitably your number will be up.
Suffering is only good because it proves that you care.
❤️‍🩹❤️‍🩹❤️‍🩹❤️‍🩹❤️‍🩹

author: @VudrokWolf
text:
I know we all leave this world one day, but sometimes we forget that even those we admire most aren’t eternal. Robert’s enthusiasm and passion inspired me deeply, the way he approached his work, whether in research, experimentation, or fabrication, was pure dedication.

For nearly nine years, I followed his videos closely. Each one was a joy to watch, full of new data, fresh ideas, and his relentless curiosity to improve and refine his results. He always encouraged others to experiment, to take notes, to repeat and discover things for themselves.

I remember when he began building his company, bringing others along with him, not just to grow his project, but to help those people grow too. That’s who Robert was: generous, curious, and driven by purpose.

I’ll truly miss him, the mentor and grandfather figure I never had. Rest in peace, Robert. Your spark lives on in all of us who learned from you.

author: @BuildingPersephone
text: Just want to add my condolences to Robert and Patti’s family and friends. If he were still around to read this, I’d say to him what an inspiring and likeable person he is. I’d say that he didn’t just help me, and millions of other people understand engineering and scientific principles in a practical way, but that he demonstrated what it means to be an engineer, to fix the world, to see in a different way, to value practicalities and knowledge, and most importantly to do it with stoicism, a wry sense of humour, and a twinkle of curiosity in your eye. In watching your last video I saw a certain sadness in your eyes that had I not known what had transpired already, I would maybe have just mistaken for the wistfulness of a sincere goodbye. And I suppose that is what it was in a way. Your energy, your character, your unique impact on the community you have built will live on without you in a generation of engineers, makers, and various curious handy-folk. You will be the humble bellweather we measure ourselves against. Good luck and good night, I hope you’ve found Patti somehow, somewhere, and I hope that your family will find peace and some way to understand your pain and decisions.

replies.text: What I mean is that he says he is stopping because there are no more topics to do, being too repetitious etc. That wasn't the reason. It was because he had things planned. author: @mikefriend1134
text:
Hey Rob! Others have beat me to everything I was going to say, but I think it all bears repeating:
You've been (and always will be) a source of hope, solace, and inspiration for me :') I think it was during the pandemic that I found this channel, and I've come back to it many times, always coming away happier than I came in. Your ingenuity, curiosity, and gentle humor have reminded me that people are largely good, and there's a real chance we might figure our way out of the messes we've got ourselves into. You've introduced me to countless cool new (or, largely, old!) gadgets and gizmos, and inspired me to do some tinkering of my own. 
I agree with others that I hope you'll stop by again, now and then. But you've done your bit and then some, and whether we see you again or not, I hope life will always send you plenty of reasons to smile:)

I know I speak for many people when I say that you are one of the treasures among the human race!

author: @dydeyaus6433
text: Dear Mr Murray-Smith,  thankyou for being you. You have taught and inspired so many people around the world and left us all a wonderful legacy. I like to imagine my dad (an electrical engineer whom I never knew) would have been much like you. Thankyou for sharing your wealth of knowledge and genuine good nature with all of us and also for  your  grace in bidding us farewell . May you be blessed with peace and joy in all of your future endeavours, and if you ever feel inspired to share anything more with us, you will always be welcomed with open hearts and minds. Farewell kind sir.

text:
Robert I am gutted. Yours was my favourite science channel, you had created a legacy which I am sure your students could continue.
I wanted to communicate with you many times, because I am a retired physicist and college lecturer and was interested in your experiments with graphene reinforced plastics.
I wanted to know how whey protein could be mixed with plastic materials because oil and water don't mix. 
I have directed so many of my online students to your channel.
You are the best sir.

author: @samuel5823yo
text:
I’m a new viewer. 1 month. I’ve already decided you have some amazing content.

Maybe you’ll come back and make AI videos. I have ADHD so everything gets boring. It helps to toss things up. One time I worked as an elevator operator for 6 months. Sometimes busy. A lot of times not. I learned how to make enjoyable times with the different people working there. At first i couldn’t deal with the boring. 

I’ve learned a lot about leadership and learning in the military. Life is full of opportunities to learn. Good luck to you, in whatever choices you make 👍 I look forward to learning from your videos I haven’t watched yet. Also, I think the algorithms on YouTube work better than they used to. Probably due to AI.

I say that because your views are 50k on day 5. I’m not a YouTube expert but I am a full on computer geek nerd. I don’t know if it’s right or not to say don’t give up on making videos just when the algorithms might be improving a lot.

author: @NickSeee
text: I understand Rob, I really do, but I have to admit that this video has made me inexplicably sad.  That's how much you've become a part of all our lives I suppose.  Ages ago you mentioned being involved with our government regarding energy policy I think. I hope they have someone with your smarts and ability to find simplicity in complex systems now. Especially when I look up at the power lines and imagine all the phase complexities involved in maintaining a national grid with so many unsynch'd renewable supplies and unpredictable loads.  Just a few degrees of phase difference between there being 240 volts give or take 10% and there being a voltage collapse.   Anyway, what I really wanted to say was THANK YOU for the last 10 years, you are a diamond geezer.  All the best to you, Nick.

author: @ghostindamachine
text: Robert, thank you so much for your efforts in making and gifting us masterclasses in engineering. Through your videos, you've made complex topics accessible and taught them in a way that anyone from every walk of life can understand. Your enthusiasm and clear explanations have demystified subjects that many people find intimidating, turning them into fascinating and approachable areas of knowledge. I believe you've inspired countless people to pursue technical hobbies and even careers they might never have considered before. Your work has shown that engineering and science aren't just for those with formal education in these fields. They're for anyone with curiosity and a willingness to learn. Speaking on behalf of many subscribers to your channel: please keep your channel up so that your videos can continue to be enjoyed and viewed by many more people for years to come. Your content is a valuable resource that deserves to remain accessible to future learners and makers. You are truly appreciated. Thank you again! Blue skies and fair winds to you.

author: @alden1132
text: Though I can't say I'm happy to hear this news, but I also would never presume to second guess your decision. Only you can know when it's time to stop, and, though I will certainly miss your content, I feel only gratitude for your time, effort, and the joy you've brought myself and (I know for a fact) many others. Part of what has always made your videos special for me has been the obvious enthusiasm and excitement you express when explaining and demonstrating the concepts and principles your have explored. Your obvious, genuine excitement to share knowledge has always been (for me, at least), a crucial, fundamental component of your videos. If you don't feel excited about the stuff you're sharing, it defeats the very justification for making them in the first place. So, I'll just say this: thank you for allowing to be a part of your exploration of these amazing projects, and for taking the time to explain them. I hope you're well. Take care of yourself, man. Cheers!

author: @jollygreenjoi
text: Ohh Robert Murray Smith thank you for your video's and introducing me to new/old wind turbine concepts like the DARWIN wind collector! These designs seem so much more appropriate than the effeciency targeting wind turbines that are built in mass which cost more to operate and maintain. Common sense seems to have gone out the window as governments the world over have been paying for and building sub-optimal configurations that cost more over time then they will ever produce. A bit sad you wont be doing any new material when it seems like there is so much more to cover in other fields displaying similar problems. The public at large is being mislead about a number of technologies and how they should or could be setup. Simple explanations and demonstrations such as you provide display the widening gap between technologies setup to benefit people as a whole or those setup to benefit one person or group of people usually in the form of power, control, or money. It's a sad state of affairs, but you have been a beacon of hope enlightening all of us on the road to a better understanding.

author: @dogsareGodsgift
text:
Sad to see another thing come to an end. Soon everything else will aswell.

This is a reminder of why everyone should get right with God. You never know when something bad happens and it might be your last time. Don't forget, we all live on borrowed time. When God decides our lease on life expires, it's over.

"The Lord is with you when you are with him. If you seek him, he will be found by you, but if you forsake him, he will forsake you." (2 Chronicles 15:2b, KJV)

"I love them that love me; and those that seek me early shall find me." (Proverbs 8:17, KJV)

But as for those who fall away:

"4 For it is impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,

5 And have tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the world to come,

6 If they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open shame.

7 For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs meet for them by whom it is dressed, receiveth blessing from God:

8 But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected, and is nigh unto cursing; whose end is to be burned."  
(Hebrews 6:4-8, KJV)

So don't forget that:

"But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved." (Matthew 24:13, KJV)

So find Jesus whilst you can before it is too late:

"Seek the Lord while he may be found; call on him while he is near." (Isaiah 55:6, KJV)

Time is very short.

The signs of the end times are very evident. That's why we know it is the season of Jesus' return.

The beast system is being built and soon they will end up enforcing the mark of the beast on everyone leading to every true Christian rejecting the mark and then being killed.

"3 And as he sat upon the mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign of thy coming, and of the end of the world?

4 And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man deceive you.

5 For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.

6 And ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.

7 For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.

8 All these are the beginning of sorrows.

9 Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall kill you: and ye shall be hated of all nations for my name's sake.

10 And then shall many be offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.

11 And many false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.

12 And because iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax cold.

13 But he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

14 And this gospel of the kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the end come."(Matthew 24:3-14, KJV)

This is a reminder that no man knows the day nor the hour. So get right with God and keep watch because if you fail you will be cast into the lake of fire and brimstone. You never know when it is your time.

Everyone has sinned and fallen short of the Glory of God and is doomed to damnation without Jesus as a result of their sins.

This means you and everyone else needs to Repent. Repenting means changing your mind about your sins and sinful nature, knowing and acknowledging what God thinks about your sins and sinful nature and coming to terms with the fact that He is right and you are wrong and you need to change to meet His Standards.

Then turn to Jesus believe in Him for Salvation and pray, ask Him to show you the the Truth, to make straight all your crooked paths and to teach you His Word in the Bible. Make sure to ask Him to help you obey the two Greatest commandments, the Greatest Commandment being to Love God above all else with all your being. The Second Greatest Commandment being to love your neighbour as yourself. If you obey these two then you will automatically obey all the Laws of God and you will be in a good relationship with God.

"For the wages of sin is death; but the gift of God is eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord." (Romans 6:23, KJV)

May God bless everyone, including those reading this comment, to have eyes that see and ears that hear so that they may turn to Jesus and believe on Him for Salvation. Repent now because the time is very short.

The March 14 blood moon and the blood rain in Iran are a sign of things to come soon.

"And they said, Believe on the Lord Jesus Christ, and thou shalt be saved, and thy house." (Acts 16:31, KJV)

author: @cragmac2
text:
Dear Robert,

I first discovered you in 2015... I had just started my PhD and was trying to work on various carbon materials for the development of biosensors and general electrochemical sensors.

I didn't have a great deal of money to help fund my research, so trying to understand how some materials were fabricated and how to do so at a low cost (whilst learning the theory) was crucial to me.

I rewatched several of your videos countless times, whilst some of the theory was initially missing, the application of the science was so important to my work, I question if I could have achieved what I did without your videos.

Whilst It is clearly sad to see you go, and I'm sure many of your viewers echo this sentiment, I greatly appreciate that you are taking the time to inform us rather than just disappearing.

Robert, it has been an honour to learn and listen to you. 

May you find happiness in whatever you decide to pursue in the future.

author: @alaingrignon
text: @Rob to say you’ve been inspiring in all those years on Youtube is a gross understatement. The knowledge you’ve imparted on all of us is tremendous. You’ve not only conveyed it in a way that anyone can understand, you’ve actually made physics, chemistry, and science in general accessible, practical, and achievable for many of us. I’ve been following you for 8 of those 11 years, I’ve been a paying member since you started and have been paying gladly. You have been the one channel I’ve watched religiously and will miss you tremendously. I do hope this is not a goodbye so much as a “see you later”. Thanks again Rob for ALL your contributions, ALL your advice, and ALL your support throughout the years. I couldn’t call myself an inventor if your channel had not existed. You made my love for invention possible, achievable, and real. God speed my friend.

author: @bearnaff9387
text:
I wish I had something unique and uplifting to say here.  Your videos were a gift not just because of what they taught us, but because of you.  Your delighted, at times almost worrying, laughter when physics just worked and a machine came to life in its own special way is going to live in my head and my heart for the rest of my life.  

That laughter...  I have to tell you, until this video I was almost sure that one day you were going show how to combine the elements of a dozen-dozen videos into a singular machine that did... something.  My bet was always that it would extract more energy from its own motion than the work used to motivate it - not necessarily perpetual motion, but some device that cunningly tapped into some physical feature of the universe to extract work in a way never seen before - like using the expansion of the universe to eventually rotate the axle of a generator.  Your videos always felt like the best kind of long con, a carefully lain trap in a debate with the universe like a garden path that lead to an unsettling implication.

That will always be who you are to me.  A man cheerfully looking at the edges of mechanical engineering with a delighted laugh that pushed back the edges of what was possible in a non-fantastical manner.  Than you for being here.  Thank you for being you.

Bloody hell Rob! Where did that come from?.......you're my daily source of knowledge/entertainment/Tinkercad and I look forward to checking/watching everything you put up.....don't know what to say......I've put so many people onto videos on your channel and your subjects are often rich sources of discussion. I remember coming across you by means of a YT happy accident donkeys years ago and messaging you about a community funding system for you to mass produce 'diy' batteries for all of us 🙂.

Being selfish I would like you to be there forever but if you have a life plan and another path you have to follow then you must pursue it. We're only here once buddy and so make the most of it. Thanks for the enthusiasm and entertainment and teaching (especially Tinkercad) and common sense and fairness and, perhaps more importantly, the sowing of the seeds of ideas and thinking.

Can't imagine not having you 'around', in my opinion there's no one else like you on YT.

Take care.

author: @fuller-media
text:
Should old acquaintance be forgot,
and never brought to mind?
Should old acquaintance be forgot,
and auld lang syne?

Chorus
For auld lang syne, my dear,
for auld lang syne,
we'll take a cup of kindness yet,
for auld lang syne.

And surely you'll buy your pint cup!
and surely I'll buy mine!
And we'll take a cup o' kindness yet,
for auld lang syne.

Chorus

We two have run about the hills,
and picked the daisies fine;
But we've wandered many a weary foot,
since auld lang syne.

Chorus

We two have paddled in the stream,
from morning sun till dine;
But seas between us broad have roared
since auld lang syne.

Chorus

And there's a hand my trusty friend!
And give me a hand o' thine!
And we'll take a right good-will draught,
for auld lang syne.

Chorus

author: @gabeebersohn5281
text: Dear Robert, You have been my hero over the last two years. I'm apart of the younger generation, mid twenties now. Growing up me and lots of my generation have been mentally led astray. never having enough solitude or time without stimulation to cultivate creativity and the ability to think deeply. When I finished school my brain simply stopped working, as it was no longer being challenged. Working on my own engineering project alongside some inspiration from your content has saved my life and my mind. If there is any way to keep up with some of your projects from here on out, please let me know. If not then farewell Robert, and keep doing great things  behind the curtain :).

It seems very often I'd search YouTube for a video of a niche but of information and you would pop up in the results. 

It's going to take a lot of people to fill the gaps in my searches in the future.

You will be missed.

author: @MrMatthewPR
text:
I think this was a long time coming to be honest. Notwithstanding the loss you endured, the channel has seemed to take a step back from the inventive and had way too much 'sponsored' (fancy tool review) content. Which frankly, would have bored me into quitting as well...
I wish you all the best going forward, but hope that you might be back with the occasional project build, refinements to your awesome concepts, and more like your enthusiastic past videos.

author: @philnicholls4954
text:
Hi Robert. Just another name on the list from your perspective, but you've been a friend for me for the past few years. I found your videos shortly after my dad died and they filled the void of wonder and interest which had been in my regular conversations with my dad. Thank you for that. I wish you well and would be delighted to see the occasional video pop up again, if you feel like it. Take care. Phil.
> A brief edit after hearing the sad news of the subsequent video from David.
I'm sorry that Rob had been struggling to the extent to make such a decision. 
My thoughts are with his family and friends.
Thank you to David for informing us.

author: @Absfor30
text:
😢

Robert, I dont know what has influenced this decision, but I do hope from the bottom of my heart that you are OK and if you need to speak to someone that you have an strong support network in place.

Whether you ever post a video again or not I want to say thank you for your curiosity, your wit, your time and your infectious laugh throughout the years. You have helped to open my children's eyes and we'd all sit round the TV and watch your videos, they said you were better than their school teachers!

From all of us here, we are devastated to see you go, but wish you well for whatever you choose moving forwards.

He's said that it's his last video so many times and phrased it in every way you can think of that now whenever he says something like  " this is it folks, that's all folks , this is the end of the line, I can't do it anymore......"  ;  then many comments follow saying like  "so long Scotty, it's been great knowing you, see you again tomorrow" .

*_All the best Robert._* For a decade now the idea of rummaging through the treasure trove that is your catalogue would occasionally pop into my head and I'd spend a glorious weekend afternoon basking in your enthusiasm and knowledge covering a wide range of topics—from clever little projects to the unassumingly, quietly profound. You've kindled my own enthusiasm for so many things over the years and I can't think of a more valued gift.

*_Thanks for all the effort and time you've given to us over the years._* I hope you know how appreciated it all is.

author: @wakayama1991
text:
Robert, over the years, you have been a source of interest, fascination and insight both to me and my son. My son is disabled, physically, but there's nothing wrong with his noggin. Wish the same could be said for his father, but I digress. 
Your enthusiasm and curiosity is contagious. My son caught it. Next year, all being well and going to plan, he will enrol in the prestigious Royal College of Engineering in the country we live in, where he (at the moment) plans to specialise in robotics. 
In no small way did you, personally you, nurture this seed of curiosity and wonder within him. I'm immensely proud of him, with all the struggles he has had in his life, and I'm immensely grateful to you. 
You have blown breath on the tinder of the imagination and minds of many more than you can ever realise. Your work here on youtube will be just a small part of your legacy, but of great import in of itself.
Thank you.

text: Oh Sad for us but Happy for You. I am so thankful for You and the inspiration you've provided me over the last few years. Your influence on my mind and life helped me start a business and take on a whole new adventure. I can tell you how much your joyful attitude has lifted me in my own hard times and I will not assume the sacrifice you made still sharing and inspiring us through you hard times. Thank You, Thank You again and again. Please enjoy your new stage and be blessed beyond imagination. Also know there will always be many people here for you any time you need. You have shown me an excellent example of sharing and community that I will aspire to leave people better than you found them. Thank You 1000000 times and more

replies.author: @peterling1422
replies.text: My hear breaks after watching Daves video sharing the sad news. As Dave mentioned this was posted the day after his passing. Rob would never have got to see what I posted..... Oh how I wish he had, I'm not sure my comment would have changed anything but regardless of him reading this I felt this and knew this long before I ever posted this comment. If you're reading this please know there are people who Love You and think You are Brilliant! weather you are ever told or not, regardless if they make it into your comments section or not. I am honestly so sad and I can't believe how much this is affecting me and I'm ok with it. To Roberts friends and family my heart goes to you and I can't imagine how you feel, I know 1 thing about grief which is please don't judge yourself by thinking "I should" or " shouldn't" feel this or that way. I think grief could be proportional to love so the more you love the more you might hurt at this time, this is the risk of loving someone which makes it a beautiful. If you didn't judge yourself for loving Robert you should not judge yourself for how you feel loosing him, it hurts, it sucks and it's ok to feel that way until one day you don't. Robert for a while was a part of my everyday, I look forward to watching everything from the beginning and continuously hoping to catch the joy he shared through curiosity thinking and tinkering. I have been changed by Robert, I hope his legacy continues to change people and I hope to carry some of his character to uplift and share with others in honour of Robert. Thank You to the viewers that gave him platform and motive, Thank You to his friends that shaped and encouraged his character But mostly Thank You to his family that shared him with us and supported him while he did so sacrificing the joy of being with him for the time he spent lifting up people he and you will never meet Thank You so very much for your kindness in sharing this gift.

Thanks to your clear and simple way off showing and explaining, so many technical things and practical know how have become available and do-able.

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text: All things come to an end . I'm quite aware of it  , but this still comes as a shock , since I've been watching this space for about ten years (since videos made in the kitchen , with very bad sound). I clicked on "thumb up" button on this video , although I feel like I should have done the opposite . It is just my selfish feeling of loosing something that I got used to for a long time , so I feel sad. Of course , I also understand your decision , life is too short to be doing anything that one is not enjoying and making videos require our most precious resource - time . Bare in mind , watching videos also requires time and that is coming from someone who watched almost all of your videos (accept one about 3D printing and printers). So , I hope that you will not be a stranger to your own channel (as many people already commented) and that you'll "swing by" occasionally  , to let your faithful audience know , how you are and if some bright idea comes to you , to present it here. You made a legacy on you tube , for which I'm very grateful (as many other people , I assume) and for all your time and knowledge , that you shared with us. I wish you all the best in the future , for very long time . You will be missed .

author: @craigbradbury
text:
Well this was unexpected,  but I'm sure you have well considered reasons for doing so. 

Just wanted to say thank you for all the videos to date.  In a sea of noisy, attention seeking, inaccurate and annoying media content, your channel was (and is) a welcome breath of fresh air. 

You're a born teacher and your content has not only helped give me the confidence to go and build stuff, but also brought the right words of encouragement and reason I've needed, seemingly just when I needed it. It's not all been about gear ratios. 😊

Whatever it is that you plan on doing next, I wish you all the best with it. This channel is a fantastic repository of info and ideas that I'm sure many will continue to enjoy and learn from for years to come. 

(Unless of course social media or society in general collapses and you'll have you take your work back underground and start a small resistance unit armed with 3d printers, gaffa tape and the ability to rewire a series 2 land rover with a spoon.)

Biggest of hugs to you, kind sir. X

author: @FunwithHydroponics
text:
Robert, maybe you could reconsider.  Your decision doesn’t need to be final.  Maybe you’ll get the urge to reconnect with us intermittently in the future or through some other medium.  

Although most viewers never subscribe or comment — You probably know that you have been a tremendously positive influence in so many of our lives.  Your interest in so many various areas has fostered a contagious fascination for the sciences in our minds.  Your creativity and enthusiasm have taught us that we too can think, experiment, fail, then learn & succeed in our own endeavors – even without an engineering or science degree.  

Your YT library is an amazing repository for us and our future generations.  However you have special gifts and talents; and I believe that you are too young in heart & mind to completely stop sharing. I hope that you will not fade into retirement and become silent.  Regardless, THANK YOU for being such an inspiration in our lives!

I just want to thank you for sharing your vast knowledge with us in all your posted videos over the years. I also want to thank you personally for the inspiration that you gave me to start my own channel and delve into my own battery research which I am still very active in and still getting immense pleasure doing. I will definitely miss your timely posts and infectious enthusiasm. I am also happy to hear that we will still have access to all that valuable information you have posted during that time.Thank you for that! Good luck and best wishes in your future endeavors.

author: @mark.guitar
text:
You have helped many of us to cope with difficult stuff simply by being there and making absorbing content.
You have inspired many of us to faff about with ideas, and bits of engineering related stuff.

Having had to retire from teaching I get that you need to stop this at some point. Though sad to see you stop posting, I am happy that you are able to make a sensible decision about the timing.

No pressure, but "Christmas card" post would be a nice thought, if you feel able to take the dust covers off for that!

I wish you all the best and appreciate all of the hard work you have put into producing such an entertaining and engaging work that I have referenced to many pupils over the last decade.

Cheers mate!

Thank you, Robert, my friend.

The rest of me wants to convey how magnificent I think your efforts have been. Fantastic work, and I am so glad to have experienced so many of your videos. Farewell, though it would be fabulous if we could see you again.

author: @stevennope8864
text: Would you be willing to do,  or pay someone to, consolidate your videos in a downloadable video library?  I, and I'm sure many others, would love the opportunity to download and save them, just to have for future references. I'd be willing to pay for that level of access of course, given that it would remove the residual income from my views. I don't know how much that would be worth, but I hate to consider the possibility of the channel and videos being removed eventually at the whim of YouTube. I imagine that YouTube will someday remove videos from their servers if they lack a set number of views after a given period of being posted.

author: @simonjarvis7362
text: Thank you Robert. It seems somewhat inappropriate to 'like' this video but I have done anyway to keep the algorithm happy! All the best to you for the future! EDIT: Some days later having learned the sad truth of what has happened I can only express my deepest sympathies to the family. Robert's grief is palpable in his last message. I only hope that through it all he understood what an inspiration he was to us 'thinkers and  tinkerers' out there in the wider world. His breadth of knowledge was not only extraordinary, it was accurate too, backed up by a solid catalogue of projects that he meticulously prepared to help bring his mini lectures to life. Robert, a man who made a difference.

author: @FuhrChris
text:
My place is making very slow progress.
I have always hoped that I would one day build some project that needed more space than you can provide in England.
I am working on making a building that will support several forms of Windmill in that the very shape of the building is designed to compress the air flow into a ridge way and house the tools needed to create the very parts that are up on the roof.
So DO KEEP IN TOUCH!
You will be given partal credit for the Build that is Not yet completed.
(I crashed the mini bike in the last 300 feet of a round trip of 160 miles.  The need for my leg to heal is slowing down the construction of the $15,000.00 worth of materials that I went and fetched.  Weather here is getting cooler and I'm concerned about trying to get things like primer, paint, and epoxy resin on before it gets too cold.  Along with that is framing up the more than 41 pieces of lumber in prep for Winter here.  
I only have a portion of the needed materials for a 30' ridge way build.  The Widow's Walk that will be capped over with enough space for the placement of the Squirrel Caged generator, you can stand on; But the roof caps that will set on at least 4' high framing (creating the empty box shape for the fans to go into) will have to wait till next Spring before they are poured Fiberglass with two part Styrofoam cores.)
Again let me say that I really could use your involvement in the projects future.

author: @totherarf
text:
This is not the video I wanted to ever see, but I understand your point!
Can I just say the most important two works in the English Language ...... Thank You!

You have allowed and encouraged me to play with the best toy I have ever played with, my mind!
You have reminded me of stuff I learned 50 years ago but since allowed to atrophy into forgetfulness. You have showed me things that are so obvious I had no clue I was not already aware of them and things that were new to me and started a cascade of ideas. Almost all the time you did this it was with a seemingly childlike fascination and enjoyment in passing this knowledge on!

Can I ask one favor of you? 
If, in time you get a hankering to do another video, you do it. Do not say to yourself "No, I can't" 
If you never get that hankering just know ..... It has been a blast, matey!

author: @Franckie.G
text:
Robert, I’ve always been impressed by how you kept publishing so many videos, never lowering the quality, even through difficult times. That in itself is something exemplary.

I’ve often gone back to many of your videos, and I will keep doing so the variety and depth of what you’ve shared has been precious. For someone like me, who had to stop school early, what you and the maker world have given is a way to keep learning, and to keep alive an optimism that would otherwise have died out.

For that, I owe a lot to people like you. If I had met you in real life, I would have simply shaken your hand and said: thank you, professor.

author: @VicNorth777
text:
The end of era! What a joy it has been learning so much from such a brilliant man. Not only because of the depth and breadth of material, but also because of the accessible presentation. You’ve touched the lives of millions, and that’s an awesome feat. 
All good things come to an end, and it’s best to appreciate all that was than to bemoan what could still be. Thank you Rob. 
Life can be difficult at times, tragic too. We can empathize and encourage each other and I hope you can rest comfortably in the knowledge we all care deeply about you and wish you all the best.
May God grant you the strength, peace, and endurance to complete the race set before you, wherever that takes you. Bon voyage… (it’s not April first, is it?)

replies.text: @Milkex yes, thanks.  I had a suspicion he was headed in that direction, but apparently this video was posted/released after he passed (not sure how that happens technically, but his brother mentioned this).  It's heartbreaking to think he skipped seeing the outpouring of love and encouragement this video received.  It's all very sad, what a lovely person he was. 😥  I do have most of his videos saved, lots to review as I embark on projects.  Think it'll be a little strange watching them knowing he's left.  I'll always enjoy his videos, but it will be mixed with sadness now.
